commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And when Samuel rose early to meet Saul in the morning, it was told Samuel, saying, Saul came to Carmel, and, behold, he set him up a place, and has gone about, and passed on, and gone down to Gilgal. Here we behold what a change prosperity makes in the manners of those who before showed the greatest humility. Saul erects a monument to his own vanity. Hebrew, "he has set him up a hand "(as Absalom did, 2 Kings xviii. ) or "a place "to divide the booty, (Jonathan) or "a garrison "to keep the country in subjection. (Calmet) Perhaps he erected the figure of "a hand "as an emblem of strength, and in honour of Benjamin, "the son of the right hand "of whose tribe he was.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
